items.headline: NATO member closes airspace to allied military over Middle East operations
items.body: Spain barred US aircraft involved in Iran operations from its airspace, extending an earlier ban on American use of joint military bases. The move tests alliance cohesion as Washington pledged to defend the NATO member while Madrid calls the war illegal.

items.headline: Australian states split on free transport as fuel shortages spread from strait closure
items.body: Victoria and Tasmania waived public transport fares through mid-year as Iran's grip on Hormuz drives energy costs higher. Federal government halved fuel excise while some stations report empty tanks despite stable national reserves.

items.headline: Africa and Latin America advance trade cooperation to bypass traditional partners
items.body: Trade ministers at WTO meeting proposed regular summits between regions representing nearly 90 countries and over two billion people. Initiative aims to boost South-South commerce currently at just 0.3 percent of global trade.

items.headline: Washington partially lifts four-month asylum freeze while maintaining ban for 40 countries
items.body: Homeland Security resumed processing applications from most countries after November pause affected four million cases. Ban remains for travel-ban nations including Cuba, Venezuela, Haiti, Afghanistan, and Iran.
